Name: Sanitation and cleanliness requirements for roadway and sidewalk cafes.
Title: A Local Law to amend the administrative code of the city of New York, in relation to sanitation and cleanliness requirements for roadway and sidewalk cafes

History.Description: This Introduction was Introduced by Council
This Introduction was Referred to Comm by Council to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure
This Introduction was Hearing Held by Committee
This Introduction was Laid Over by Committee

Summary: This bill would require roadway and sidewalk cafe operators to ensure that their outdoor setups are clear of trash, debris, graffiti, vermin, food scraps and unsanitary conditions. Operators who violate this provision would be subject to civil penalties ranging from $300 for the first offense to $600 for the second and subsequent offenses.
Text:
Be it enacted by the Council as follows:
      
      
      Section 1. The table of maximum civil penalties in section 19-150 of the administrative code of the city of New York is amended by adding a new row in numerical order to read as follows:
Section of the Administrative Code
Maximum Civil Penalty (dollars)
19-160(b)(3)
300 (first violation); 600 (second and subsequent violations at the same place of business.
      � 2. Subdivision b of section 19-160 of the administrative code of the city of New York is amended by adding a new paragraph 3 to read as follows:
      3. A sidewalk and roadway cafe shall maintain clean setups that are clear of trash, debris, graffiti, vermin, food scraps, and unsanitary conditions. The area of the roadway extending one and one-half feet distance beyond any roadway cafe on all sides of such cafe shall be kept clean.
      � 3. This local law takes effect 120 days after it becomes law.
